message:
fix(runtime): panic-safe I/O driver, real uring detection, reduced lock contention in worker

io_driver.rs: Introduce RAII `Restorer` drop guard in `restore_and_extract_wakers`
to guarantee the `Events` buffer is returned to the `IoDriver` even if an
`on_event` closure or waker clone panics mid-iteration. Previously, a panic
between extracting the buffer and the manual restore at function end would
permanently leak the events buffer, causing all subsequent I/O polls to fail.

reactor/linux.rs: Replace ~90 lines of non-functional placeholder structs
(`IoUringReactor`, `EpollReactor` with every method returning
`Err(Unsupported)`) with a thin alias module that re-exports from the real
`epoll` and `io_uring` implementation modules. Eliminates dead code duplication.

reactor/uring.rs: Replace hardcoded `is_available() -> false` stub with real
runtime capability detection. On Linux, reads `/proc/sys/kernel/osrelease` to
check kernel >= 5.1 (minimum io_uring support) and reads
`/proc/sys/kernel/io_uring_disabled` to respect the sysadmin disable flag
(available since kernel 6.1). Non-Linux unconditionally returns false. Test
updated from asserting false to platform-conditional contract check.

scheduler/worker.rs: Refactor I/O polling from `try_lock` + `turn_with`
(holding mutex during blocking epoll_wait) to `try_turn_with` (atomic
`is_polling` leader election, mutex held only briefly to extract/restore events
buffer). This eliminates a lock-contention bottleneck where other workers were
blocked from registering/deregistering I/O sources while one worker was in
epoll_wait (up to 1ms). Time snapshot and trace setup moved outside the lock
scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
